settings.adminDescription: Rewrites player chat to show their name and rank in their rank colour (overridden by Rank-in-Name).
Puts the rank tag inside the player's name (e.g. '[ACM] Brick') so native chat and text-to-speech still work; overrides Reformat when on.
Replaces a whole message with a canned line if it contains a racist slur; ordinary swearing is left alone.
Points awarded to every player on the winning side at match end.
Bonus points for the top scorer of the match.
Bonus points for the second-highest scorer of the match.
Bonus points for the third-highest scorer of the match.
Seconds between full per-player stats snapshots emitted to the bot.

How many times per second the server engine updates - higher = snappier, more accurate AI & missile reactions (not physics), at more CPU cost. Default 60. TO APPLY: save it, then run  run.bat --rewrite-wrapper  and restart the SERVER (drops players). A plain restart keeps the old rate until the wrapper is rewritten.

PvP only: keeps the two teams' sizes close so one side doesn't badly outnumber the other - if a team is more than the 'Max Team Size Gap' ahead, extra players are blocked from joining it (and with Auto-Move on, the best-fit player is moved to the smaller side).
How many players one side may be ahead before balancing kicks in (2 = a 2-player gap is fine, only a 3+ gap acts).
PvP only: when a side is too far ahead, actually move the best-fit player to the smaller side (off = block-join only).

settings.friendlyName: Only Move Players in Spawn Menu
settings.adminDescription: Auto-balance only moves players who are not currently flying (sitting in the spawn menu).
Seconds between auto-balance checks.
Minimum seconds between two auto-balance moves, to stop churn.
Legacy/unused: old silent hold before a move, superseded by Warn Seconds.
Auto-balance never runs unless at least this many humans are on; small lobbies are left alone.
After teams go unbalanced, broadcast a warning and wait this many seconds before moving anyone (default 5 min).
Once moved, a player won't be moved again for this many games, spreading the burden around.
Never move a player who joined less than this many seconds ago (default 15 min); strongest balance immunity. 0 = off.
Auto-balance by NuclearSkill rating instead of server rank when choosing who to move.
Maximum players in a !squadup group; squadmates get a weak balance immunity.
How long a !squadup invite stays open for the invited player to accept with !y.
PvP matches: every player starts at at least this in-game rank (funds/loadout floor). 0 = off; PvE unaffected.
PvP only: lets a team vote to surrender the match via !forfeit (majority needed).
Seconds before a team can start another forfeit vote (anti-spam); the vote window is min(60, this).
Altitude in metres the brief Cricket is spawned at during !swapteam/!forceteamswap before ejecting; raise to 3000 if a crash is seen.
Comma-separated SteamIDs allowed to use in-game team commands (!move/!spec/!join/!balance).
Suppresses the spammy native AI kill feed and instead announces kill streaks and carrier/destroyer sinks.
NuclearSkill points added to that life's score for capturing a base.
NuclearSkill points added to a winner's final life at match end.
NuclearSkill points added to a loser's final life at match end.
PvE co-op: when the mission timer runs out and humans haven't won, declare them defeated instead of silently rotating.
Periodically despawn lingering ejected pilots on the map to cut clutter and load.
Seconds a dismounted pilot may linger before being cleaned up.
Auto-punishes friendly fire: 1st = eject+warning, 2nd = kick+rank reset, 3rd = persistent ban.
Performance guard: caps AI aircraft and clears stuck ones; only ever removes AI, never players.
Max AI aircraft flying per faction; the excess (grounded/lowest first) is destroyed.
Max total aircraft (AI + players); when exceeded, AI is removed from the busiest side, never a player.
A grounded AI that hasn't moved for this many seconds is cleared to free a clogged runway. 0 = off.
Movement radius in metres under which a grounded AI counts as 'not moving' for the stuck check.
Rate-limits fleet move-orders to stop order-spam mass-disconnecting the lobby; needs a server restart to apply (gates a patch).
Sustained fleet move-orders accepted per second per player; a human issues well under 1/s.
Max burst of fleet orders before excess is dropped (token-bucket capacity); first orders are never dropped.
Logs (throttled) the name/SteamID of any player whose flooding orders are being dropped.

Defence-in-depth: silently drops RPCs aimed at already-destroyed objects to kill a disconnect-storm amplifier; needs a restart (gates a patch). Applies live; if the patch failed to bind at load it will not re-bind without a server restart.
Points given to every player present at kickoff (fires at the 1-minute mark); bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Seconds into a new mission that still count as 'start of match' for the kickoff bonus; bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Bonus points for downing an enemy player in PvP; bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Extra kill points per player your side is outnumbered by, rewarding the underdog; bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Mission length in seconds (default 3h); must match the server's MaxTime. Bot constant, needs a bot restart.
How long the end-of-match map-vote ballot stays open (seconds); bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Length of the !votemap yes/no approval poll (seconds); bot constant, needs a bot restart.
Enable/disable the game's built-in player vote-to-kick (via run.bat --set-votekick); applies on reload-config / next mission / restart.

PvP only: when the mission timer runs out with no winner, the team with the higher TOTAL in-game score wins (an exact tie is a draw) instead of rotating with no result. Off = just rotate. No effect in co-op/PvE.

Fire the timeout result (PvE defeat / PvP score result) this many seconds BEFORE the mission's MaxTime, so the match ends early enough for the map vote to run before the game auto-rotates. 120 = 2 min early; 0 = exactly at MaxTime.

Which units players may move via CmdSetDestination. HeliDroppedOnly (default, recommended) = ONLY player-deployed ground vehicles (Hexhound SAM/GMG, AA, APC, LAC SAM/AT, AT trucks, radar truck, Jackknife) -- blocks mission/AI ground units, ships, missiles. All / RateLimitOnly = any commandable unit (more lenient -- can allow command-flooding). AllowlistTypes = ground vehicles by jsonKey (Command.AllowedJsonKeys). Disabled = no commanding. The per-player rate limit + anti-grief auto-kick always apply on top. Live-tunable.

Only used when Policy=AllowlistTypes. Comma-separated UnitDefinition.jsonKey values to allow (case-insensitive). EMPTY = all ground vehicles. Turn on Command.DiagLog to discover the exact jsonKeys in BepInEx/LogOutput.log.

Log the resolved unit type (Class/jsonKey), player-deployed state, and ALLOW/DROP for each command order to BepInEx/LogOutput.log. Turn ON briefly to discover unit jsonKeys / confirm what's blocked, then OFF (verbose).

Anti mass-DC (Layer C): raise Mirage's per-connection reliable-send-buffer cap so a transient command/RPC burst is absorbed instead of overflowing into a BufferFullException that disconnects everyone. Applies at the next match host. Leave ON.

Layer C target for the per-connection reliable-send-buffer cap (game default 3000). RECOMMENDED RANGE: 8000-24000 for normal play; 12000 (4x) is the default and handles a busy server. Go higher (e.g. 48000+) only as a stopgap for a known high-unit-count server; 999999 effectively removes the ceiling (uses more memory per connection). Clamped >=3000, never lowers an already-higher value. Applies at the next match host.

Anti-grief: automatically KICK a single player mass-commanding units to brick the server (the buffer flood that mass-disconnects everyone) -- kicks the OFFENDER, not the lobby, and files a report in the Reports tab. Two-factor (see RequireActiveFlooding) so legit base-builders aren't kicked. Off = no detection/report/kick.

Only used when 'Require Active Flooding' is OFF: then a player merely OWNING more than this many live ground vehicles is auto-kicked. With Require Active Flooding ON (the default) the kick is driven by the order RATE and this threshold is ignored.

Recommended ON: auto-kick on sustained move-order flooding ALONE (>= Flood Order Rate, held ~4s), regardless of how many units the player owns -- catches command-spam even on units they don't own. OFF = also kick anyone simply OWNING more than the Owned-Unit Threshold (aggressive; can false-positive a base-builder).

The sustained move-order rate (orders/sec per player, held ~4s = 2 scan windows) that auto-kicks a single connection spamming commands. A legit commander is well under 1/s and the game caps accepted orders at ~5/s, so 3/s sustained = deliberate macro/click-spam. LOWER = more aggressive (3 catches '3+/s on one unit'); raise toward 5 if you see false positives.
If ON, a tripped offender is also BANNED (kicked on rejoin), not just kicked once. Default OFF = recoverable kick; you can always ban from the Reports tab.
If ON, DETECT + REPORT to the Reports tab but do NOT kick -- validate the threshold against real play first. Default OFF = actually kick.

Recommended ON: never auto-kick a player whose SteamID is in Admin.SteamIds (an admin may legitimately mass-command units for a scenario). Turn OFF only to self-test the detector on your own admin account.

notes:
SOURCES (all read directly): plugin Config.Bind block = C:/Users/Server/Documents/Nuke Option Server/NukeStats/NukeStatsPlugin.cs lines 98-212 (44 entries total, all enumerated). Bot constants = no_mapvote_bot.py: MISSION_MAX_TIME(L67), VOTE_DURATION(L120), APPROVAL_DURATION(L121), KILL_BONUS(L159), UNDERDOG_PER_PLAYER(L160), WARN_THRESHOLDS(L218, not surfaced as a single tunable - see below), START_BONUS_PTS(L225), START_BONUS_WINDOW(L226). Game config VoteKick = DedicatedServerConfig.json VoteKick.Enabled, set via no_mapvote_bot.py set_votekick() (L3626, run.bat --set-votekick on|off).

COVERAGE: 43 plugin Config.Bind entries + 7 bot constants + 1 game setting = 51 settings. I omitted WARN_THRESHOLDS (a Python LIST [3600,1200,600,300,60] of warning times, not a single scalar - not suitable for a single int/float control; mention to user if they want it editable as a comma list). All other requested bot constants are included.

LIVE vs RESTART: Every plugin ConfigEntry is read live each HQ tick EXCEPT Flood.Enforce and Flood.DropDeadNetIdRpcs - those gate Harmony patch bindings at load (the CmdSetDestination throttle patch and the private-Mirage RPC patch), so toggling them needs a server restart; their numeric params (PerSec/Burst/LogDrops) ARE live. ALL bot constants are Python module-level = require a BOT restart. VoteKick is a game-config JSON change applied on reload-config / next mission / full restart (marked restart).

RECOMMENDED GROUP ORDERING (top = most touched):
1. Match  2. Team Balance  3. Scoring & Ranks  4. AI & Performance  5. Squads  6. Moderation  7. PvE  8. Chat  9. Flood Guard
(Put Flood Guard and the live=restart items last since they're rarely touched and some need a restart.)

RECOMMENDED SIMPLE (curated) VIEW - ~14 settings, based on owner Tomo's actual change history:
1. MISSION_MAX_TIME (mission length, ~3h) [bot]
2. AILimit.PerTeamAICap (32) [plugin]
3. AILimit.TotalAircraftCap (64) [plugin]
4. Balance.Enforce (autobalance on/off) [plugin]
5. Balance.MinPlayers (6) [plugin]
6. Balance.WarnSeconds (300) [plugin]
7. Balance.NewJoinerSeconds (900) [plugin]
8. START_BONUS_PTS (250) [bot]
9. KILL_BONUS (50) [bot]
10. Scoring.WinPoints (200) [plugin]
11. PvE.TimeoutForceDefeat (off) [plugin]
12. DedicatedServerConfig.VoteKick (off) [game]
13. Mission.PvpStartingRank (3) [plugin]
14. Teamkill.Enforce (on) [plugin]
(Stretch to 15 with Squad.MaxSize or Chat.ProfanityFilter if room.)

UI CAVEATS to surface to the admin:
- Flag bot-owned + game-owned + the two Flood patch toggles as 'needs restart' in the UI (live=restart) - changing them via cc_web won't take effect until the bot/server restarts. Plugin live settings apply on the next HQ tick and persist to BepInEx/config/anz.nukestats.cfg on Config.Save().
- Balance.GraceSeconds is LEGACY/unused (kept for completeness, commonlyChanged=false) - consider hiding it.
- Admin.SteamIds is a comma-separated string, not a single ID. Min/max omitted for string/toggle types by design.
- WARN_THRESHOLDS (mission-time chat warnings) is a list and not in the catalogue as a scalar; expose as an editable comma-separated field if desired.
